its a quote from the poem Ophélie by Arthur Rimbaud

> Ô pâle Ophélia ! belle comme la neige !
> Oui tu mourus, enfant, par un fleuve emporté!
> C’est que les vents tombant des grands monts de Norwège
> T’avaient parlé tout bas de l’âpre liberté;

"O pale Ophelia! beautiful as the snow!
Yes, you died, child, by a river swept away
For the winds falling from the great mountains of Norway
had whispered to you of harsh liberty"

But also, regarding the illness, I find it interesting that personality also seems to affect the symptoms experienced - even with the kids, where lust isn't really a factor. Like, Ogier, who is very emotional, has the heat increase and the breathing issues, but with Tulu, who is more rational-minded, the MC doesn't notice anything (though granted, they only have contact with his tentacles, which might not be affected). And given that the way Tulu plans to resolve the situation is by waiting it out at the Black Pool (so, y'know, with Tawil), there's a clear indicator that closeness to the concept of Rationality/Logic can mitigate the effects

embeds.title: Niko's Event Unlock Recommendations
embeds.description: Since the in-game side story list is not in chronological order, this is a spoiler-free list of side stories I recommend to those interested in the lore of Morimens to read and in what order.  This list is also a spoiler-free companion list to my (spoiler full) Morimens timeline project, where I ...

content: apart from the obvious "watch story in order"
niko wrote this for side stories that are recommended
Nice, going to construct a quick list to make it easier for myself but this is very helpful!

Side note: I just finished the Murphy candy ghost story and this actually has huge lore implications too

While spinning her yarn for the random trick-or-treating kids, Murphy not only mentions mythag university, but talks about "field missions" implying that the paramilitary adventurism of mythag investigators is common knowledge

However, the story she tells is an exaggerated depiction of Dissolution symptoms, and she clearly doesn't expect the children to recognize it. This implies that Dissolution is not common knowledge (which we already know, more or less, not to mention it's self-censoring)

So what do the locals who live near mythag think? They have to know about the mist wall right? What is the logistics situation like for mythag, do they have to turn off the mist wall when the building maintenance crews come?

The locals don't know about the mission statement of mythag, so what do they think the mythag investigators are doing? Do they think it's a supernatural place? What kind of area 51 urban legends do they have about it
This is such a crazy rabbit hole
They probably think it's some kind of government lab
with magic
if you've finished ch9 there's also the full timeline sheet you can reference

Mythag has a whole private area around the school
so I don’t think there are local people around there

Ok, I think this is a reasonable summary of what mythag looks like

[The Black Pool with the clock tower or whatever]
[research laboratories]
[state-of-the-art infirmary and surgical ward]
[investigator, keeper, student, awakener dorms]

[a small town's worth of servant housing and mundane employee housing]
[day-care facilities for children]
[cafeteria]
[kitchen]
[kitchen garden + chicken coop and whatever else]
[probably also food stalls, a cafe, a pub, and the like]
[probably a carpenter, cobbler, tailor, blacksmith]
[laundry for 500 people]
[electric generator, probably multiple]
[granary and preserved food storage for 500 people]
[multiple fresh water wells and probably a water tower]

---THE MIST WALL---

[dense forest]
[confusing mess of roads in the countryside]

[connection to londinium road network]
